methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Westmag is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Hansen and Jordan Sanders that has raised $11M to date. The target sector includes nine comparable entities: one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers spanning Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity, among others.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the $11M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $28M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient narrow peer set specificity within robotics and manufacturing at matching stage or valuation tier; while seven same-sector comparables exist, fewer than three datapoints met adjacent-stage or similar-tier filters, making sector-wide median application unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, with a median of $28M. This range reflects a ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. Primary uncertainties center on the absence of statistically robust peer multiples at identical stage and valuation tier, and reliance on a single methodological input rather than triangulated consensus. Capital efficiency metrics, revenue traction if any, and burn rate relative to runway would strengthen conviction in this range.
